subject: Chemistry
title: Spontaneous Combustion of Hay
description: A 60-second lesson on Spontaneous Combustion of Hay.

hook.text: Wet hay stored in a barn can burst into flames by itself. No spark needed.

buildup.text: Bacteria in damp hay start decomposing it. This biological process slowly releases heat.

discovery.text: If the hay is packed tight, heat can't escape. Temperature climbs past 150°C and ignition begins.

twist.text: This destroys barns worldwide every year. Farmers must dry hay below 20% moisture to prevent it.

climax.text: Spontaneous combustion is real—not supernatural. It's biology producing heat that chemistry ignites.

punchline.text: Wet hay, trapped heat, no spark. Bacteria start the fire.

quiz.question: What causes hay to spontaneously combust?
options: Bacterial decomposition generates heat that can't escape
